top_stories.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
top_stories.summary: Cisco has acquired Astrix and WideField to add Non-Human Identity (NHI) security capabilities to its security stack. The acquisitions position Cisco to secure machine identities and agentic AI workloads by treating identity as the primary control plane.

top_stories.title: Robinhood Cuts Access Approval Time to Support High-Velocity Development
top_stories.summary: Robinhood's engineering security team overhauled its system access approval process to reduce approval times while maintaining security for developers. The initiative focused on streamlining access granting workflows to support faster development cycles in a fintech environment.

top_stories.title: He Thought He Was Secure; His Phone Number Was Stolen Anyway
top_stories.summary: The article covers SIM swap attacks as a method for stealing SMS-based one-time passwords, leading to account takeovers. The piece highlights the vulnerability of SMS-based MFA and the need for stronger authentication methods.

top_stories.title: Proof’s x401 establishes an open protocol for AI agent identity and authorization
top_stories.summary: Proof has launched x401, an open issuer-neutral protocol designed to handle identity verification and authorization for AI agents interacting with websites and APIs. The protocol allows services to request and verify credentials covering identity, organizational affiliation, signing authority, proof of humanness, and other claims before permitting agent actions.

top_stories.title: Mirage2FA phishing kit uses HTML smuggling to steal Microsoft 365 credentials
top_stories.summary: Researchers at Fortra identified the Mirage2FA phishing kit, which uses HTML smuggling and obfuscated JavaScript to deliver fake Microsoft 365 login pages that steal credentials during MFA prompts. The campaign employed business-themed lures such as secure documents, remittance services, and payment requests to trick targets into interacting with the malicious attachments.

news.title: Ukraine Says Russian Intelligence Used Fake Support Texts to Steal Messaging Credentials
news.summary: The SSU and FBI uncovered a Russian intelligence campaign targeting messaging account credentials of government officials, military personnel, politicians, and activists across Ukraine, Europe, and the U.S. The operation used fake support texts as a social engineering vector to steal messaging account credentials from high-value targets.

news.title: SIM-swapping gang busted in international police operation
news.summary: Polish authorities arrested four suspected members of a cybercrime group responsible for SIM swap attacks, cryptocurrency theft, and money laundering, in coordination with the FBI and HSI. SIM swapping is a method used to bypass SMS-based multi-factor authentication by hijacking victims' phone numbers.

news.title: Amazon Q Developer Flaw Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code via MCP Configs
news.summary: A high-severity vulnerability (CVE-2026-12957, CVSS 8.5) in Amazon Q Developer allowed malicious repositories to execute commands and steal cloud credentials through MCP server configurations. Amazon has patched the flaw, which required a developer to open a malicious repo and trust the workspace for the exploit to trigger.

news.title: Guardian Agents: The Next Layer of Identity Governance
news.summary: The article examines how AI agents operating in enterprise environments create governance gaps in existing identity infrastructure, which was built for human access management. It addresses the challenge of governing autonomous actors that inherit permissions and traverse systems at machine speed without adequate oversight from current IAM programs.

news.title: Truist Securities Reiterates a Buy on SailPoint (SAIL), Keeps the PT
news.summary: Truist Securities reiterated a Buy rating on SailPoint (SAIL) stock on June 17, maintaining its price target. This is an analyst ratings update for the identity governance vendor, with no new product or company operational details disclosed.

news.title: Proof launches x401, an open protocol for verifying who’s actually behind AI agents
news.summary: Proof has launched x401, an open protocol designed to verify human authorization behind AI agents during digital transactions. The protocol aims to establish identity verification standards for AI-driven interactions, addressing the emerging challenge of authenticating who controls autonomous AI systems.

news.title: Microsoft integrates Entra and Purview to secure AI agents and data
news.summary: Microsoft is launching a webinar series focused on integrating Microsoft Entra and Microsoft Purview to address identity, access, and data protection challenges in AI environments. The initiative targets management of sensitive data flowing through AI prompts and agents, combining IAM and data governance capabilities.

news.title: Poland busts SIM-swapping gang tied to millions in crypto theft
news.summary: Polish authorities arrested four members of a cybercrime group that conducted SIM-swapping attacks by breaching telecommunications partners and hijacking email accounts. The attacks resulted in millions in cryptocurrency theft, exploiting mobile identity verification mechanisms.

news.title: Webinar: Why account takeovers remain one of the hardest threats to stop
news.summary: A webinar addresses the persistent challenge of account takeover (ATO) attacks, which are difficult to detect because attackers operate through legitimate accounts and trusted services. The session focuses on using behavioral AI to identify compromised accounts and automate response workflows.

news.title: Britive Joins Torq AMP Alliance Program to Accelerate Agentic SecOps
news.summary: Britive has joined the Torq AMP (Alliance and Marketing Program) to integrate its runtime Privileged Access Management capabilities with Torq's agentic AI security operations platform. The partnership focuses on technical integration, go-to-market collaboration, and joint marketing efforts.

news.title: Virtru and Rocket.Chat Partner to Bring Real-Time Access Governance to Classified Collaboration
news.summary: Virtru and Rocket.Chat have partnered to integrate real-time access governance into classified collaboration environments. The integration synchronizes channel membership with identity entitlements, automatically revoking access when user permissions change.

news.title: Keeper Security Brings Privileged Access Controls to Microsoft Teams With New Keeper Teams App
news.summary: Keeper Security has launched a new app that integrates its privileged access management capabilities directly into Microsoft Teams. The Keeper Teams App enforces zero standing privilege and provides governed, auditable workflows for credential access within the Teams environment.

news.title: Flare unveils new CTI capabilities and Okta support
news.summary: Flare has announced expanded capabilities for its CTI (Cyber Threat Intelligence) platform, including a new Okta integration within its Identity Exposure Management (IEM) offering. The updates extend identity threat intelligence into agentic workflows and tactical threat intelligence use cases.

news.title: Web社内報サービスSOLANOWA、IdP連携の新オプション「ユーザー自動同期（SCIM）」を提供開始
news.summary: Japanese web intranet service SOLANOWA has launched a new "User Auto-Sync (SCIM)" option for IdP integration. The feature enables automatic user provisioning and synchronization between identity providers and the SOLANOWA platform.

news.title: Amadey and StealC Malware Network Disrupted, 27M Stolen Credentials Recovered
news.summary: A coordinated law enforcement operation involving Europol and private sector partners including Microsoft disrupted criminal infrastructure behind Amadey and StealC malware, recovering 27 million stolen credentials. The operation targeted cybercriminal "assembly lines" used to harvest credentials for ransomware, financial fraud, and critical infrastructure attacks.

news.title: Navigating the hidden costs and governance risks of Microsoft 365 E7
news.summary: Microsoft launched Microsoft 365 E7 (branded as the Frontier Suite) in May 2026 at $99 per user per month, bundling E5 capabilities with Microsoft 365 Copilot and the Entra Suite for advanced identity features. The article examines the hidden costs and governance risks associated with this new licensing tier, which has direct implications for identity and access management through its inclusion of the Entra Suite.

news.title: Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data
news.summary: Attackers breached application vendor Klue and used its stolen OAuth tokens to access and exfiltrate customer data from Salesforce. The attack vector highlights OAuth token security risks, with the scope expanding as additional victims are identified.

news.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
news.summary: Threat actors deployed a Golang-based credential sniffer targeting 430,000 FortiGate firewalls, identifying 110 million credentials in an ongoing global campaign. The attack exploits FortiGate vulnerabilities to turn network devices into credential harvesting tools, directly compromising authentication data at scale.

news.title: FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
news.summary: A Russian-speaking initial access broker conducted a large-scale credential-harvesting operation called FortiBleed, targeting over 430,000 FortiGate firewalls globally and collecting 110 million credentials. The campaign, active since February 2026, involved collecting credential lists, brute-forcing accessible systems, and targeting exposed services on FortiGate devices.

news.title: Trump Order Sets 2030 Deadline for Federal Post-Quantum Crypto Migration
news.summary: President Trump signed Executive Order 14409 on June 22, requiring federal agencies to migrate high-value assets and high-impact systems to post-quantum cryptography by December 31, 2030, with digital signatures deadline set for December 31, 2031. National security systems are placed on a separate migration track under the order.

news.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Highlights Software Development Pipeline Risk
news.summary: A GitHub token leak at Novo Nordisk exposed vulnerabilities in software development pipelines related to secrets management. The incident illustrates how organizations misclassify secrets management as a tooling issue rather than an identity and access management problem.

news.title: FIFA Bug Exposes World Cup Streams to Remote Takeover
news.summary: A security vulnerability in FIFA's systems allowed potential remote takeover of World Cup streams due to unenforced Microsoft Entra access controls. The bug demonstrated how misconfigured identity and access management controls could expose critical broadcast infrastructure to unauthorized access.
